summary: GH-102 spec is approved for a minimal Core consumption fixture contract. The spec keeps scope limited to a Lode-side repo-local fixture and discoverability metadata for package admission and schema validation. It explicitly excludes Core/Harbor/App code, Core result envelope schema, runtime/live evidence, hosted registry, and write behavior.
reviewed_validation_summary: Review refresh after PR #130 gate classification: product implementation head `9cfa003ea3a10200936b5253a6b7f8001df32035` contains only GH-102 scoped changes; local validation passed (`python3 tools/lode_validate_package.py sites/example/read-public-page --json`, explicit registry-index validator, `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m py_compile tools/lode_validate_package.py`, `jq empty`, GH-102 fixture `jq -e`, `git diff --check HEAD~2..HEAD`, `loom fact-chain`, `loom suite validate`, `loom suite carrier validate`, and `loom review read`). Hosted run `28545559519` had `py-compile`, `demo-bootstrap`, `repo-local-cli`, and `loom-check` pass; `loom-pr-merge-gate` failed as review/validation-summary drift, so this carrier refresh realigns review and latest validation summary without product changes.
follow_ups: GH-103 must document deferred write guardrail behavior before parent FR #89 can close.
